title: chamaecytisus albus subsp. microphyllus
intro: Chamaecytisus albus subsp. microphyllus, also known as white broom or mountain broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a shrub that is native to the mountains of southern Europe and western Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory ailments, and has been shown to have antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its potential health benefits. Chamaecytisus albus subsp. microphyllus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional medicine to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.
